rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TEDMAN MARK
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Remaining procedural issues: 1) Paragraph 15 of the instant petition states that an election pursuant to Probate Code Section 13502 will be filed by the surviving spouse prior to the hearing, but no such election has been filed. 2) Paragraph 23 alludes to a declaration supporting an extraordinary fee request by petitioner's counsel, but no such declaration has been filed. A petition for extraordinary compensation must include, or be accompanied by, a statement of facts upon which the petition is based (CRC 7.702). 3) The petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed to each distributee, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) to be distributed to each beneficiary. (LRSF 14.35.I.6.a). 4) A petition for distribution must list and describe in detail the property to be distributed, in the body of the petition or in an attachment that is incorporated by reference (CRC 7.651(a)). 5) No proposed order has been received (LRSF 14.7.A). 6) A petition to establish a special needs trust for Jameson Mark has not been filed. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DAVID C. GUTEKUNST LIVING TRUST EXCECUTED june 25, 2004, as amended and restated on Apr 14 23
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions Amendments And Restatement
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Lindsay Brennan, who initially objected to this petition, filed a request for dismissal of her objection without prejudice on 10/01/2024. The proposed order has been revised to request an order "appointing" Kelley Lee and Katherine Preston as successor co-trustees. The petition has not been amended to request this additional relief, and it is unclear whether the petitioner has agreed to resign as trustee in favor of immediate appointment of Kelley Lee and Katherine Preston as presently acting co-trustees of his revocable trust. The Court is to determine whether paragraph 3 of the proposed order should be modified to clarify that Kelley and Katherine are merely designated as successor co-trustees, but their actual appointment and authority to act as trustees will be subject to amendment or revocation by the petitioner. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DUNCAN LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/27/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) If the statutory fee is based on any amount other than the I&A, detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sales must be included. [CRC 7.705(b) and 7.550(b)(6).] (2) A petition for final distribution must state the DATE on which notice was mailed to the FTB. [LRSF 14.35 I.2.] (3) Attorney costs must be itemized. A lump sum of $105 for filing fee costs without more is insufficient. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 11/15/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (It seems unlikely that cash in the amount of $256,972.76 grew in little over a year to $374,346.13...)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANCIS-JOSEPH MASSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/26/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) An amended pleading requires the same notice of hearing (INCLUDING PUBLICATION) as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] No proof of publication of the notice of the AMENDED PETITION to administer the estate has been filed. (2) Mailed notice must also show that an AMENDED PETITION was filed. (3) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as information about remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (4) Paragraph 8 of the supplement is illegible, but it appears to indicate that Item 9 of the amended petition should have indicated "0" pages attached. There were several pages attached to the amended petition. (5) Item 8 of a petition for probate must show the name, AGE, address, and the relationship to the decedent of each person listed. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by 11/15/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Issues should just be addressed in a supplement or an amendment to a petition (or a completely amended petition) that stands on its own, not "answering" issues as in a test. For example, responding "Correct" in a filed document, when tentative rulings are not part of the filed record, makes no sense. There is also no need to narrate that notice will be mailed; it should just be mailed and a courtesy copy of the proof of service provided to the court.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM LLOYD SIEWERT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/26/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No will file has been provided to the examiner. (2) What is the authority for the Court to admit a DEED as a will? (3) The decedent's son post-deceased him and no personal representative has been appointed yet for William Aaron Siewert's estate. (4) Michelle von Jaeger has no standing to waive bond if she has not yet been appointed as the personal representative of William Aaron Siewert's estate. (5) It appears, from the petitioner's declaration filed on 9/10/24, that the petitioner could claim that he would have been adopted by the decedent but for some legal barrier. [Probate Code Section 6454.] Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 11/15/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE MARRIAGE OF PAUL PERCY PINTO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF MARRIAGE
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
